geopolitics_summary: Macron-Meloni rapprochement may ease EU policy coordination; UK ETS linkage talks postponed amid Starmer resignation; China energy competition intensifying.

themes.headline: Europe faces intense LNG bidding competition from China and Asia
themes.why_it_matters: Direct TTF risk. If Asian buyers outbid EU on spot LNG cargoes, European winter fill shrinks, pushing forward curve higher. Structural margin compression for gas-fired generation.

themes.headline: EU-UK ETS linkage deal postponed following Keir Starmer resignation
themes.why_it_matters: Delay to EUA-GBP spread arbitrage clarity. Postponement of July 22 summit defers emissions trading mechanics between blocs; uncertainty weighs on EUA valuation and cross-border hedging.

themes.headline: Fossil fuel lobby targets EU methane reduction rules amid energy crisis
themes.why_it_matters: Policy risk to future gas supply cost. If methane rules weaken, upstream capex efficiency may degrade, tightening near-term EU gas import terms. Indirect TTF upside if regulatory relief stalls.

themes.headline: Europe faces extreme heat stress amid heatwave and infrastructure limits
themes.why_it_matters: Immediate demand surge and hydro constraint risk. High temps boost AC load; simultaneous cooling-water scarcity for thermal plants reduces capacity utilization. DA spreads and Cal+1 power curve shift higher intraday.

themes.headline: UAE exit from OPEC+ reduces cartel crude production and leverage
themes.why_it_matters: Indirect. Smaller OPEC+ cohesion weakens crude price floor, reducing oil-to-gas switching incentives in EU. Long-term Brent softness may lower fuel-oil gen economics, sustaining gas burn.

watchlist: EU-UK ETS linkage summit rescheduled (new date TBD post-Starmer transition).
Methane rule lobbying intensity — watch for Commission softening June–July.
LNG import auction results and Asian buyer participation — TTF arb signal.
European heatwave impact on hydro inflows and thermal plant derate; monitor water temps.
